preferences.label: Traceability is the product
preferences.description: Every piece of output traces back to its source — page number, scan coordinates, OCR engine, confidence score, processing step. Data without provenance is noise.

preferences.label: Fidelity to the source
preferences.description: The system preserves what the document contains, not what it thinks the document means. OCR errors are bugs. Formatting changes are bugs. Missing content is a catastrophic failure.

preferences.label: Any format, any condition
preferences.description: Scanned PDFs, born-digital, images, mixed layouts, degraded quality. No format is unsupported. The system adapts to the input, not the other way around.

preferences.label: Zero configuration
preferences.description: The user's job is to provide a document. Everything else — format detection, pipeline selection, quality validation — is the system's job.

preferences.label: Dossier-ready output
preferences.description: The output format serves Dossier's ingestion requirements. Structure, provenance, and metadata are first-class. The converter's job ends when Dossier can consume the output without transformation.

preferences.label: Transparency over magic
preferences.description: When something goes wrong, the system shows you exactly what happened: which page, which module, which decision, what confidence.

preferences.label: Graduate, don't accumulate
preferences.description: Converters that are proven and stable should move to Dossier. Doc-forge stays lean — it's always working on the next unsolved problem, not maintaining solved ones.

requirements.label: Ingest
requirements.description: Accept documents in any common format (PDF, images, text, office documents) and produce a normalized internal representation.

requirements.label: Detect
requirements.description: Identify what the document is and what conversion strategy it needs. Format, quality, layout complexity, content type — all assessed automatically.

requirements.label: Extract
requirements.description: Produce clean, accurate text. OCR quality must match or exceed the best human transcription. Layout must be preserved where it carries meaning (tables, multi-column, structured data).

requirements.label: Illustrate
requirements.description: Detect, crop, and catalog every illustration with precision. Whitespace trimmed, captions preserved, overlapping text excluded.

requirements.label: Structure
requirements.description: Decompose the document into semantic parts with provenance. Every piece of output knows where it came from.

requirements.label: Validate
requirements.description: Prove the output is correct and Dossier-ready. Every page accounted for. Text accuracy verified. Structure validated against expectations.

requirements.label: Export
requirements.description: Produce output in Dossier's expected format with full provenance metadata.

stories.blocker_summary: After Story 192 corrected the Alverson transcript to the visible front-page boundary, the current checked-in LOC handwritten proof pair still cannot honestly clear the `0.99` handwritten bar in this repo state because the maintained rescue seam remains below bar on both real fixtures and no fresher stronger OCR substrate has been verified on the corrected corpus.
stories.blocker_evidence:
- Story 192 corrected the Alverson transcript to the exact visible front-page boundary and reran the maintained handwritten corpus, writing `benchmarks/results/handwritten-notes-story192-alverson-frontpage.json` with `pass_rate = 0.6`, `overall_min_ratio = 0.677267`, and `page_min_ratio = 0.677267`. The six synthetic rescue cases still passed at `1.0`, but the real fixtures stayed below bar: Barney scored `0.908567` on image entry and `0.886568` on PDF entry, while corrected-scope Alverson scored `0.677267` on image entry and `0.681952` on PDF entry.
- Manual artifact inspection on that corrected rerun confirmed the remaining issue is OCR quality on visible source content, not hidden continuation text. Alverson artifacts at `output/runs/handwritten-handwritten-notes-alverson-real-image-handwritten-rescue/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` and `output/runs/handwritten-handwritten-notes-alverson-real-pdf-handwritten-rescue/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` still misread the visible front page with errors such as leading `nothing`, `Battle of Chickamauga`, `lates novels`, and `excepatable`. Barney artifacts at `output/runs/handwritten-handwritten-notes-barney-real-image-handwritten-rescue/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` and `output/runs/handwritten-handwritten-notes-barney-real-pdf-handwritten-rescue/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` still drift on names and phrases such as `Harlen`, `genesee`, and `father has had gone`.
- Story 191's fresh direct `gpt-5` / `claude-opus-4-6` probes remain only historical negative evidence because they were measured before Story 192 corrected the Alverson transcript surface. They did not produce a winning OCR substrate then, and no newer corrected-corpus stronger-model winner has been verified since. That keeps this story blocked on missing OCR capability rather than on missing paperwork.
- 2026-04-05 bounded `/improve-eval handwritten-notes-transcription` rechecked the retry gate after verifying current model availability with `python scripts/discover-models.py --check-new`. Fresh maintained rescue reruns under `benchmarks/results/handwritten-notes-improve-eval-20260405-barney-baseline.json` and `benchmarks/results/handwritten-notes-improve-eval-20260405-alverson-baseline.json` reproduced the current blocker instead of revealing silent drift: Barney stayed at `0.883604` image / `0.756036` pdf, and corrected-scope Alverson stayed at `0.677267` image / `0.681952` pdf. The fresh image-only subject-model screen at `benchmarks/results/handwritten-notes-improve-eval-20260405-image-screen.json` also failed to produce a winner. `gpt-5.4-pro` wrote empty `page_html_v1` artifacts at `output/runs/eval-barney-image-gpt-5-4-pro/01_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` and `output/runs/eval-alverson-image-gpt-5-4-pro/01_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` (`overall_ratio = 0.0` both), which is current integration/pipeline-wrong evidence rather than a usable OCR result. `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` improved Barney only to `0.843434` at `output/runs/eval-barney-image-gemini-3-1-pro-preview/01_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` and left corrected-scope Alverson at `0.262102` at `output/runs/eval-alverson-image-gemini-3-1-pro-preview/01_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl`, still with the same visible-source failure class. No fresh candidate materially beat both corrected real fixtures, so this story remains blocked and should not reopen yet.
stories.unblock_condition: Unblock this story only when a materially different OCR substrate or maintained recovery seam is ready to be tested against the corrected corpus and it produces fresh current-pass `driver.py` / eval artifacts that bring Barney and the corrected Alverson front-page fixture to `overall_min_ratio >= 0.99`, `page_min_ratio >= 0.99`, and `pass_rate = 1.0`. If a future story intentionally widens Alverson to the full two-page letter, that fixture-scope work must happen first and be rebaselined separately before it can count as OCR evidence here.

That resolves the prior source/transcript mismatch for the repo-owned one-page fixture, but the OCR lane still misreads visible front-page text, so this remains a real-slice OCR failure rather than a hidden continuation-text blocker.

Story 189 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]`; Story 192 kept that one-page wrapper unchanged, corrected the transcript scope to the visible front page, and reran the maintained handwritten rescue lane to `0.681952` on PDF entry. The bounded handwritten Gemini rescue seam is materially better than the historical generic low-0.1 Barney baseline, but it is still not stable or general enough: Barney continues to drift on names and phrases such as `Harlen`, `genesee`, and `father has had gone`, while corrected-scope Alverson still shows visible-page OCR misses such as the spurious leading `nothing`, `Battle of Chickamauga`, `lates novels`, and `excepatable`.

Story 192 resolved the truth-surface blocker for the repo-owned Alverson front-page fixture by trimming the transcript to the visible source boundary. The handwritten lane is now blocked by OCR capability on the current real fixtures, not by an internal source/transcript mismatch. A future two-page Alverson benchmark would need a separate back-page asset and transcript update instead of silently widening this fixture.
Messy cursive, degraded notes, and broader real-world handwriting collections remain unproven.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to one checked-in ZIP fixture with three launchable direct-entry members and one explicitly blocked unsupported member.

The maintained lane does not yet claim direct folder input, PDF-member routing, grouped image-member interpretation, nested archives, attachment extraction, ambiguous AI-assisted route decisions, or broad heterogeneous archive ownership.

Mixed-archive provenance currently relies on archive-relative `member_path` / `member_id`, extracted member paths, and downstream run ids or artifact paths; it does not flatten members into one combined bundle or claim archive-level page anchors.

sections.title: Recommended Order
sections.markdown:
Guiding priorities: keep bounded intake + `doc-web` claims honest and widen reusable format coverage only when substrate and proof exist.

Use `docs/methodology/graph.json`, `docs/methodology/state.yaml`, and `/triage` output for current sequencing. Story `205` now stands as the completed bounded proof for the first `mixed-archive` ZIP seam; use it as evidence for archive-member manifest/member-route behavior on the checked-in fixture, not as a blanket archive-support claim. Story `191` remains blocked and should still be treated as a health flag rather than evidence of a currently buildable OCR lane. Historical Fighting Fantasy/gamebook stories remain in the index below as project record and reference evidence, not as the default mission queue.

methodology_tooling.label: Methodology Tooling
methodology_tooling.notes: Stories 199 and 204 are the current methodology line: compiler hardening is done, and the remaining work is keeping state, graph, and generated-index truth aligned with later repo changes.

intake_and_routing.label: Intake and Routing
intake_and_routing.notes: Story 194 fixed the office recommendation boundary, Story 200 added the first web-page direct-entry seam, and Story 205 closed the first ZIP-only mixed-archive routing slice. The bounded archive/member manifest plus direct-entry dispatch substrate now exists for one checked-in ZIP fixture, while direct folder input and broader archive semantics remain intentionally out of scope until separately proven.

ocr_and_extraction.label: OCR and Extraction
ocr_and_extraction.notes: The real handwritten line is now an explicit OCR-capability blocker: Story 192 corrected the Alverson truth surface, and Story 191 remains parked until a materially stronger OCR substrate appears.

document_structure_and_consistency.label: Document Structure and Consistency
document_structure_and_consistency.notes: Story 198 deleted retired crop retry and validator residue after the bounded crop simplification line; no additional structure/runtime cleanup is currently due in this domain.

doc_web_runtime.label: doc-web Runtime
doc_web_runtime.notes: Recent bounded runtime work widened maintained proof across XLSX, born-digital PDF, PPTX, EPUB, EML, and MBOX. The runtime boundary is currently honest; future cleanup should wait for a new cross-format seam rather than re-auditing the just-landed direct-entry slices.
